text: Worked through harvesting proven patterns from the spun-off openreview repo (and the working directory) back into the core living-workspace, focused on the dashboard and its server. The central decision was that the dashboard server belongs with the dashboard skill as one unit â€” the skill/server should ship together as a reusable "kit" rather than being split apart. After the user asked to see and approved the proposed structure, the standard kit was built into template/: tools/repo.py as a data-access skeleton (path/load/save/next-id conventions with empty KINDS and PROJECTIONS registries marked as grow-here points) and tools/server.py as the generic dashboard server exposing index at /, templates at /view/<name>, registered projections at /api/<name>, plus /api/_index and /health. Finished with a repo-wide consistency sweep verifying the canon-wraps-narrower relationship held word-identical across template, canon, and example, and left a memory note for future sessions.
